excerpt: He was fasting during the war that day and had been hungry for the last two days.
source_work: KASHKOLE, Binder 12 (Biography of Prayers and Events)

excerpt: Ali said, 'Surely, my lord! But there is a clear difference between the two of us, and that is that if you weigh the two parts of any person I have killed, you will always get the same weight.'
source_work: Rahat al-ʿAql (attributed to al-Kirmānī); oral tradition preserved in Ismaili hagiography
source_location_hint: Spiritual station of the Imām versus the greatest companion

excerpt: He smiled and said, 'Brother, I am not angry with you. That is why I came to the mosque to offer prayers and ask for forgiveness for you.'
source_work: KASHKOLE, Binder 12; Nahj al-Balagha tradition (al-Sharif al-Radi)

excerpt: O ʿAlī! You shall meet Uwais, and you shall recognize him by two signs: a light radiating from his palm, and another emanating from his ribs.
source_work: Kitab al-ʿAlim wa-l-Ghulam (attributed to Jaʿfar ibn Mansur al-Yaman); al-Sahifa al-Sajjadiyya (Imam Zayn al-ʿAbidin)
source_author: Jaʿfar ibn Mansur al-Yaman / Imam Zayn al-ʿAbidin
source_location_hint: Recognition of the saints (awliyāʾ) by luminous signs

excerpt: When he arrived and clung to the feet of Amir al-Mu'minin and asked for allegiance...The Master of the Universe said, 'Awais! Your allegiance has been fulfilled in eternity, you are the eternal lover!'
Sayyidina al-Muʾayyad al-Shirazi served as the chief dāʿī during the reign of Maulanā Maʿad al-Mustanṣir bi-Llāh...the Imam bestowed upon him the honorific title 'al-Muʾayyad'.
source_work: al-Majalis al-Muʾayyadiyya (al-Muʾayyad al-Shirazi); Jamiʿ al-Hikmatayn (Nasir-i Khusraw)
source_authority: Fatimid daʿwa historiography and Ismaili philosophical tradition
source_location_hint: Chapter on the succession of duʿāt and the Fatimid institution

excerpt: Sayyidina al-Muʾayyad al-Shīrāzī stands as the first dāʿī to formalize the philosophical science of cosmogony (ʿilm al-mabādiʾ) into a systematic doctrine. Before his era, the deeper meanings embedded within the sciences of the Prophets lay concealed within the wisdom (ḥikma) of Maulanā ʿAlī.
source_location_hint: Formation of Fatimid daʿwa doctrine and the system of emanation

excerpt: Had I been born in the time of the Prophet Muḥammad, my spiritual station would not have fallen short of that of Salmān al-Fārsī himself.
source_work: al-Majalis al-Muʾayyadiyya (al-Muʾayyad al-Shirazi); biographical sources in KASHKOLE tradition
source_authority: Ismaili hagiographical tradition and transmitted accounts
source_location_hint: Self-testimony regarding spiritual rank and the trials of the daʿwa

excerpt: Your station with us is precisely that which Maulanā Salmān al-Fārsī held with the Messenger of Allāh...Had you lived in the time of the Prophet Muḥammad, You would not have been inferior to Salmān al-Fārsī!
source_authority: Fatimid Imamic correspondence and preserved poetry
source_location_hint: Exchange between Imām al-Mustanṣir and the chief dāʿī on spiritual rank
